source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
As shown in the figure, the "snail"-shaped track $O A B$ is vertically fixed on the horizontal ground and smoothly connects with the ground at point $B$. The "snail"-shaped track consists of a smooth semi-circular track $O A$ and a semi-circular track $A B$ that are smoothly connected. The radius of the semi-circular track $O A$ is $R_{1}{=}0.6\mathrm{m}$, and the radius of the semi-circular track $A B$ is $R_{2}=1.2\mathrm{m}$. The length of the horizontal ground $B C$ is $x_{B C}=11\mathrm{m}$, and at point $C$ there is a large opening leading to a deep pit. A small ball with mass $m{=}0.1\mathrm{kg}$ enters the track $O A$ from point $O$ along the tangential direction with a certain initial velocity, then moves along the track $O A B$ to the horizontal ground. It is known that the coefficient of kinetic friction between the small ball and the horizontal ground is $\mu{=}0.4$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the minimum initial velocity of the ball at point $O$ to prevent it from leaving the $O A B$ track?
2. If the initial velocity of the small ball at point $O$ is $v=6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, find the magnitude of the pressure exerted by the small ball on the semicircular track at point $B$.
3. To make the small ball fall into the pit $C$, what is the minimum initial velocity of the ball at point $O$?

image_dependency: question_text explicitly says "As shown in the figure"
track geometry, connection points O/A/B, and spatial layout to ground and pit are diagram-based
asset_registry_record shows image exists and registry_passed=true
multi_step: the sample contains 3 sub-questions rather than a single direct query
physics solution likely requires combining geometry of two semicircular tracks with energy/normal-force/friction conditions
verifiability: answer_type=short_text with explicit numeric outputs on separate lines
all three requested quantities are concrete numerical results, which are in principle checkable by physics derivation
the answer format is concise and not open-ended

decision_reasons: asset set is complete: question, answer, and primary image are all present
registry passed with no recorded issues, so there is no clear asset integrity problem
initial scoring is strong across image dependency, multi-step reasoning, and verifiability
although the problem is diagram-dependent and multi-part, the required diagram is available, so the core semantics are recoverable

As shown in the figure, the "snail"-shaped track $O A B$ is vertically fixed on the horizontal ground and smoothly connects with the ground at point $B$. The "snail"-shaped track consists of a smooth semi-circular track $O A$ and a semi-circular track $A B$ that are smoothly connected. The radius of the semi-circular track $O A$ is $R_{1}{=}0.6\mathrm{m}$, and the radius of the semi-circular track $A B$ is $R_{2}=1.2\mathrm{m}$. The length of the horizontal ground $B C$ is $x_{B C}=11\mathrm{m}$, and at point $C$ there is a large opening leading to a deep pit. A small ball with mass $m{=}0.1\mathrm{kg}$ enters the track $O A$ from point $O$ along the tangential direction with a certain initial velocity, then moves along the track $O A B$ to the horizontal ground. It is known that the coefficient of kinetic friction between the small ball and the horizontal ground is $\mu{=}0.4$, and $g$ is taken as $10\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}^{2}$.

1. What is the minimum initial velocity of the ball at point $O$ to prevent it from leaving the $O A B$ track?
2. If the initial velocity of the small ball at point $O$ is $v=6\mathrm{m}/\mathrm{s}$, find the magnitude of the pressure exerted by the small ball on the semicircular track at point $B$.
3. To make the small ball fall into the pit $C$, what is the minimum initial velocity of the ball at point $O$?

agent_assessment.rationale: The physics problem statement is fully specified, including dimensions, friction, and target quantities. The figure clearly shows the two connected semicircular arcs, the ground segment BC, and the pit at C, so the text-image pair is semantically easy to understand.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The sample is fully understandable, the answer set is complete, the rewrite is usable as a single open variant, and alignment/solvability signals show no serious issues. The image is clear enough to support the geometry described in the text.
